content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 18, 2009

Paul O. Scott
President, Antelope Valley
Community Labor Coalition
P.O. Box 5025
Lancaster, CA 93539

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-065

Dear Mr. Scott:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice dated January 17, 2006, in which you ask whether you may concurrently serve as both the president of the Antelope Valley Community Labor Coalition, a 501(c)(3) corporation, and as the Treasurer of the Coalition's political action committee. However, there is nothing in the Political Reform Act (the "Act") that prohibits you from concurrently serving in both positions and your question does not implicate the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.

Pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), the Commission may decline to provide advice where the request does not present a question under the Act. (See also, Reg. 18329(c)(4)(F).) Because your question falls outside the purview of the Act, we must decline to provide advice at this time as the Commission would not be the appropriate agency to respond to your request.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

Because your question falls outside the purview of the Act, the Commission must decline to provide advice at this time as the Commission would not be the appropriate agency to respond to your request.
sections.question_synthetic: Can an individual simultaneously hold the position of president of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization and treasurer of that organization's political action committee?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the question does not present an issue under the Political Reform Act, and the Commission is not the appropriate agency to address this matter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document dated March 18, 2009 in response to request from January 17, 2006. File number W-09-065 indicates a withdrawal or declination matter.

Because your question falls outside the purview of the Act, the Commission must decline to provide advice at this time as the Commission would not be the appropriate agency to respond to your request.

embedding.summary: FPPC declines to provide written advice on whether an individual may serve concurrently as president of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and treasurer of its political action committee, finding the question falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.
